seo.title: AI Agent Error Handling Patterns in DSPy Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix ai agent error handling patterns in DSPy agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In DSPy, AI Agent Error Handling Patterns manifest as silent failures in composable modules where optimized prompts produce brittle outputs that don't handle edge cases, leading to uncaught errors during inference. Chain-of-thought modules often generate plausible but logically flawed reasoning chains that propagate errors downstream. Module composition exacerbates this by creating unexpected output distributions that violate assertions only visible at runtime. Without explicit error handling wrappers, DSPy agents fail gracefully but unpredictably, making reliability hard to enforce.

howItManifests.scenario: Optimized ChainOfThought module overfitting to training examples
howItManifests.symptom: Module produces correct outputs on validation data but hallucinates on OOD inputs
howItManifests.impact: Agent confidence scores remain high while factual accuracy drops to 20%

howItManifests.symptom: Chain-of-thought reasoning creates plausible but incorrect intermediate logic
howItManifests.impact: Final answer wrong in 40% of cases despite high retrieval relevance

howItManifests.scenario: Signature with basic assertions on output format
howItManifests.symptom: Assertion violations occur at inference time due to LLM formatting errors
howItManifests.impact: Pipeline crashes mid-execution, no fallback mechanism triggered

howItManifests.scenario: Composed agent with GenerateAnswer + ReAct modules
howItManifests.symptom: Unexpected output distributions from module chaining
howItManifests.impact: Downstream modules receive malformed inputs, causing 60% failure rate

fixes.name: Error-Boundary Module Wrapper
fixes.description: Create resilient DSPy modules that catch and recover from common failure modes

fixes.name: Vex-Verified DSPy Pipeline
fixes.description: Integrate Vex verification directly into DSPy module composition
fixes.code:
import dspy
import vex

class VexVerifiedModule(dspy.Module):
    def __init__(self, module, assertions):
        super().__init__()
        self.module = module
        self.assertions = assertions
    
    def forward(self, **kwargs):
        result = self.module(**kwargs)
        
        # Vex verification
        with vex.verify(self.assertions) as check:
            check(result['answer'], 'is not empty and length > 10')
            check('reasoning' in result, 'contains reasoning trace')
        
        return result

# Usage
verified_qa = VexVerifiedModule(
    dspy.ChainOfThought('question -> reasoning: str -> answer: str'),
    assertions=[
        "result['answer'] is not None",
        "len(result['answer']) > 5",
        "'because' in result['reasoning'].lower()"
    ]
)

fixes.name: Robust Multi-Module Composition
fixes.description: Safely compose DSPy modules with input validation and output normalization

vexAutomation.heading: Automating AI Agent Error Handling Patterns Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex automates DSPy agent error handling by wrapping module forward() calls with runtime verification that catches assertion violations, output distribution shifts, and reasoning failures before they propagate. It integrates seamlessly with DSPy's optimizer loop, validating both training and inference outputs against declarative assertions that catch overfitting and logical inconsistencies. Vex provides automatic remediation through fallback modules and confidence-based routing, ensuring DSPy agents remain reliable even when LM outputs degrade.

The integration requires only pip install dspy vex-sdk, then wrapping your DSPy modules with vex.verify(). Vex captures full execution traces including intermediate module outputs, enabling root-cause analysis of composition failures. It automatically generates remediation code for common patterns like missing error boundaries and assertion gaps.

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Install Vex with `pip install vex-sdk` and wrap your DSPy modules to catch 95% of error handling failures automatically.
